name: Creole Eggs
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat bacon fat in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion, green pepper, celery, and garlic. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
2. Add mushrooms to the skillet. Cook for an additional 5 minutes until vegetables are softened.
3. Remove the skillet from heat and set aside.
4. In a saucepan, melt butter over medium heat. Gradually whisk in flour to form a roux. Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
5. Slowly add milk to the roux, whisking continuously until smooth and thickened. Continue cooking for another 2-3 minutes.
6. Stir in chopped tomatoes, parsley, and sautéed vegetables.
7. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
8. Arrange a layer of sauce at the bottom of a greased casserole dish. Place half of the sliced hard-boiled eggs on top.
9. Sprinkle with bread crumbs and a small amount of Parmesan cheese.
10. Repeat the layers of sauce, egg slices, bread crumbs, and Parmesan cheese until all ingredients are used up.
11. Finish with another layer of sauce, covering the casserole completely.
12. Bake for 20 minutes or until heated through and lightly golden on top. Remove from oven and let cool slightly before serving.
